title: 怪屋
summary:
Inspector Taverner and Charles discuss the motive behind the marble lion doorstop used in the attempt on Josephine's life. Taverner concludes that Lawrence Brown likely set the trap because his character avoids direct physical violence, preferring mechanical methods like the ice axe. Taverner suggests that Brenda may have plotted the poisoning of the old man, but the mechanical trap was likely Lawrence's doing. Taverner notes that the letters found will allow the prosecutor to file a case if Josephine recovers.

Following a police request, the trial is postponed. Josephine is expected to make a full recovery, and Sophia ensures that her impulsive mother is barred from visiting the child to prevent a dramatic scene. After the postponement, Gaskill gathers the Leonidis family in Margda's living room to read Aristide Leonidis's explanatory letter and his will. 

The reading reveals that Sophia has inherited the family. The family members react with varying degrees of decorum and chaos. Margda comments on the cunning nature of the will, while Margaret behaves theatrically, asking Sophia for money. Philip remains composed, rebuking Margaret for her behavior. Margda attempts to pressure Sophia into helping Roger, but Roger declines, stating that he and Clementine intend to live a simple life in the West Indies once the company collapses. Edith de Harvillan advises Roger to accept help to protect Sophia's reputation from rumors, but Roger dismisses her concerns, asserting that he and Clementine know what they want.
